description.undefined: Ontology modelling the European catalogue of medical devices and in vitro diagnostics under MDR (EU 2017/745) and IVDR (EU 2017/746) regulatory frameworks, including manufacturers, risks, incidents and field safety corrective actions registered in EUDAMED.

comment.undefined: An in vitro diagnostic device intended to be used by lay persons in a home environment.
The FSCA has been completed and closed.
An incident that directly or indirectly led to death, serious deterioration of health, or a serious public health threat.
Communication to users regarding a safety issue.
The current status of a field safety corrective action.
A country in which a manufacturer is registered or an incident has been reported.
Risk event that causes moderate harm requiring medical intervention.
Risk class under EU IVDR 2017/746.
A potential hazard associated with a medical device, classified according to IMDRF terminology.
A corrective action taken by a manufacturer to reduce risk of death or serious deterioration in health associated with a device in the field.
Medium-high risk class under MDR.
Medium-low risk class under MDR.
A device intended to be used on one individual during a single procedure.
Risk event that could directly cause death or irreversible harm.
An active device that is intended to be totally or partially introduced into the human body.
The regulatory risk classification assigned to a medical device based on its intended purpose and associated risks.
A reportable event involving a medical device that has or could have led to patient harm, as defined under EU MDR Article 87.
Highest risk class under MDR.
Risk event that causes serious but potentially reversible harm.
Lowest risk class under MDR.
The type of field safety corrective action taken by a manufacturer.
Correction of a device at the user's location.
Highest risk class under IVDR.
Medium-high risk class under IVDR.
Removal of a device from the market or from use.
A legal entity that manufactures or places on the EU market a medical device and bears responsibility for its conformity.
Low-medium risk class under IVDR.
Lowest risk class under IVDR.
The EU regulation under which a medical device is classified and registered.
A medical device as defined under EU MDR 2017/745 or IVDR 2017/746, registered in EUDAMED.
EU Medical Device Regulation 2017/745.
EU In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation 2017/746.
A medical device intended to examine specimens derived from the human body for diagnostic purposes, as defined under IVDR 2017/746.
The FSCA is still ongoing.
An active medical device intended to be totally or partially introduced into the human body.
A medical device intended to be totally or partially introduced into the human body and to remain there after the procedure.
Health Products Regulatory Authority - Irish competent authority for medical devices.
Bundesinstitut für Arzneimittel und Medizinprodukte - German competent authority for medical devices.
The national regulatory body responsible for supervising medical devices in a given EU member state.
Notice to users with instructions to avoid or mitigate risk.
The degree of harm or impact associated with a risk event.
A medical device whose operation depends on a source of energy other than that generated by the human body.
Risk class under EU MDR 2017/745.
The clinical use for which the device is intended.
Relates a medical device to its registered manufacturer.
The unique identifier of the manufacturer.
The sex of the patient involved in the incident.
The date on which the FSCA was closed.
Relates a manufacturer to the national competent authority that supervises it.
The date on which the FSCA was initiated.
The unique identifier of the incident.
Indicates whether the device can be used more than once.
A textual description of the entity.
Relates a risk to its severity level.
The Unique Device Identifier (UDI-DI) assigned to the device.
The unique identifier of the medical device.
The unique identifier of the field safety corrective action.
The date on which the incident event occurred.
The legal name of the manufacturer.
Relates a manufacturer to the country where it is registered.
The name of the risk as defined in the IMDRF terminology.
Relates a medical device to the EU regulation under which it is classified.
The unique identifier of the risk.
The commercial name of the medical device.
The official registration number issued by the competent authority.
Relates an FSCA to its current status.
The regulatory contact email of the manufacturer.
Relates an incident to the risk that materialised.
Indicates whether the device is implantable.
Relates an incident to the medical device involved.
The EUDAMED reference number for the FSCA.
Relates an incident to the manufacturer that reported it.
The IMDRF terminology code identifying the risk type.
The number of devices affected by the FSCA.
The age of the patient involved in the incident.
Indicates whether a corrective action was taken following the incident.
Indicates whether the device requires an energy source to operate.
Relates a field safety corrective action to the incident it addresses.
Indicates whether the device is an in vitro diagnostic device.
Relates an FSCA to its type of corrective action.
The clinical outcome of the incident.
A description of the devices or lots affected by the FSCA.
The Single Registration Number assigned in EUDAMED.
The date on which the incident was reported to the competent authority.
Relates a medical device to its regulatory risk classification.
